name: Gamberi in Padella (Garlicky Pan-Roasted Shrimp)
recipeIngredient: or 1 whole hot pepper, dried, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the dark intestinal veins from the shrimp. Make each cut deep enough to butterfly the shrimp so they can be opened flat like a book.
2. Fill a bowl with ice water and add sea salt. Add the butterflied shrimp to the bowl and let stand for 15 minutes to bring out some of the briny flavor of the shellfish (Removing their shells robs the shrimp of some of their natural flavor). Drain and dry thoroughly with a cotton kitchen towel.
3. In a large skillet over medium heat, warm olive oil. Add garlic and red pepper flakes and sauté gently until the garlic softens but is not browned, about two minutes.
4. Add the butterflied shrimp to the pan so they do not curl too much. Sauté, turning once, until they are opaque, about two minutes on each side.
5. Add wine and stir for an additional 30 seconds to allow alcohol to evaporate. Remove and discard whole peppers if using.
6. Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
Variation with tomato: Add 1 small ripe tomato, peeled, seeded, and coarsely chopped, or 2 canned plum tomatoes, seeded and coarsely chopped, to the sautéed shrimp in the skillet. Stir to mix, then add wine and proceed with the recipe.
